rulings.Case Title: WENDY SHARP VS. A STAR HOLDINGS LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2025, line 12, RESPONDENTS A STAR HOLDINGS LLC, PETER ACWORTH'S Motion For Protective Order The unopposed motion for protective order is continued on the court's own motion to May 20, 2025. Moving parties A Star Holding LLC and Peter Acworth are ordered to provide a supplemental request for judicial notice attaching the supersedeas bond for which judicial notice is sought. The court does not have this document and parties are generally required to provide a copy of documents for which judicial notice is sought with the request for notice. See Local Rule 8.6. Moving parties shall provide the supplemental RJN no later than five court days in advance of the continued hearing date. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: YAN GROSSMAN VS. HIGH GROUND CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S Order On Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Plaintiff'S Requests For Production, Set One; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declarations In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2025, line 13, 2 - DEFENDANT HIGH GROUND CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S Order On Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Plaintiff'S Requests For Production, Set One Defendant's motion is untimely. Plaintiff served notice of entry of the court's order on March 28, 2025, by electronic service. The deadline for a reconsideration motion was 10 days plus two court days for electronic service, which the court calculates as April 9, 2025. The court declines to exercise its inherent power to reconsider. The prior order said that responses must be verified but did not specify whom. Melendrez v. Superior Court (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 1343, recognizes that an attorney may verify discovery responses for an entity party. (Id. at p. 1346 ["the law provides that an attorney can verify responses on behalf of a corporation"].) No leave of court is required to exercise this option, but only a willingness to suffer the partial waiver of attorney-client and work product protections that such verification entails. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: YAN GROSSMAN VS. HIGH GROUND CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S Order On Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Set No. 1, Award Of Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declarations In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2025, line 13, 1 - DEFENDANT HIGH GROUND CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S Order On Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Set No. 1, Award Of Sanctions Defendant's motion is untimely. Plaintiff served notice of entry of the court's order on March 28, 2025, by electronic service. The deadline for a reconsideration motion was 10 days plus two court days for electronic service, which the court calculates as April 9, 2025. The court nonetheless exercises its inherent power to reconsider its prior discovery order in part. Many of the facts that Defendant relies on in seeking reconsideration were put before the court in opposition to Plaintiff's motion to compel the depositions of Jack Schnell and Defendant's PMQ. Those facts provide a basis for partial sua sponte reconsideration. The court modifies its prior order to permit Defendant's counsel to sign the verification. (See Melendrez v. Superior Court (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 1343, 1351.) (It's not clear to the court that this is even a modification: responses were ordered to be verified but the order did not specify who was to verify them.) The court further extends the time for Defendant to pay the ordered sanctions to July 21, 2025. The court does not reconsider the granting of sanctions. High Ground's ability to satisfy its discovery obligations became much more difficult after Mr. Schnell became incapacitated but the interrogatories at issue were originally propounded long before those events and should have been answered in full. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: YOU LIANG SONG VS. ARTHUR LIU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2025, line 14, DEFENDANT ARTHUR LIU DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT The court's obligation is to construe second amended complaint "with a view to substantial justice between the parties." (Thomas v. Regents of University of California (2023) 97 Cal.App.5th 587, 605 [quotation marks and citation omitted.) With that standard in mind the court overrules the demurrer. The court notes that, in violation of CRC 3.1320(d), Defendant filed the demurrer on October 16, 2024 and set it for hearing nearly seven months later without a showing of good cause. This alone provides a basis to overrule it. Nonetheless the court considers the merits in the alternative. The court denies the request to take judicial notice of numerous prior court files for lack of specificity and for Defendant's failure to comply with Local Rule 8.6(A). The court construes the document filed by Plaintiff on September 30, 2024 as a second amended complaint. The court construes it as pleading three causes of action: a first cause of action for legal malpractice (appearing in the portion of the document beginning with "Part one:" and continuing through and including the paragraph that discusses contempt of court, and then including the paragraph labeled "abuse of litigation procedures"); a second cause of action for defamation; and a third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, which is how the court construes the paragraph concerning mental distress from being required to translate from Chinese. Defendant demurs on the grounds of incomprehensibility and untimeliness. The court overrules the first ground; as discussed above the causes of action are sufficiently discernible to provide notice of what is at issue. As to the second ground, the action was filed on December 15, 2021, and at least some of the conduct complained of occurred within any applicable limitations period according to the face of the complaint. Defendant's declaration stating additional facts cannot be considered on demurrer. Defendant shall respond to the complaint within 10 days as provided in Rule of Court 3.1320(j). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ASMAHAN ATTAYEB VS. MATTHEW C. MULLENWEG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Complaint Declaration Of Patrice L. Goldman
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2025, line 3, 1 - PLAINTIFF ASMAHAN ATTAYEB'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Complaint Declaration Of Patrice L. Goldman Plaintiff's motion to file an amended complaint is denied. Plaintiff filed this action on June 9, 2022. Plaintiff now seeks to add a claim for defamation against defendant Matthew Mullenweg that accrued on October 15, 2024. (Goldman Decl., Ex. C.) A pleading that seeks to allege facts that occurred after the filing of the original complaint is a supplemental complaint. (See CCP 464(a); Foster v. Sexton (2021) 61 Cal.App.5th 998, 1032 ["A 'supplemental' pleading is used to allege facts occurring after the original pleading was filed. [citation] In contrast, the additional allegations in an 'amended' pleading address matters that had occurred before the original pleading was filed."].) The weight of authority states that it is improper to add a new cause of action in a supplemental complaint. (See Flood v. Simpson (1975) 45 Cal.App.3d 644, 647 [affirming denial of leave to supplement to add a new claim]; Hebert v. Los Angeles Raiders, Ltd. (1991) 23 Cal.App.4th 414, 426 [same]; Lee v. Bank of America (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 197, 207 [criticizing Honig v. Financial Corp. of America (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 960 [allowing an employee to add a defamation claim that arose after he filed his harassment/termination complaint].) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ASMAHAN ATTAYEB VS. MATTHEW C. MULLENWEG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Production Of Documents In Proper Esi Format, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2025, line 3, 3 - DEFENDANT'S MATTHEW MULLENWEG, KATHLEEN MULLENWEG, AUDREY HC, LLC'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Production Of Documents In Proper Esi Format, And For Monetary Sanctions Hearing required. The court's tentative appears below. The court orders plaintiff Asmayan Attayeb to produce whatever correspondence with Jenniffer Westmoreland has not yet been produced, other than communications subject to the attorney-client privilege, for which a privilege log shall be provided with the production. Attayeb's statement of compliance to RFP 74 does not indicate that documents were being withheld pursuant to an objection, in contravention of CCP 2031.240(b), and asserts privilege without providing "sufficient factual information for other parties to evaluate the merits of that claim, including, if necessary, a privilege log" (CCP 2031.240(c)(1).) Production shall be made by the trial date. The court modifies its deposition order; Defendants may elect to depose Attayeb concerning the new production within five days after production is complete. The Mullenwegs seek evidentiary sanctions for Attayeb's failure to preserve call logs, which are apparently now inaccessible. The moving papers did not seek evidentiary sanctions, but the loss of discoverable writings could not have been known because Attayeb's responses to RFP Nos. 43, 44, and 50-74 did not comply with CCP 2031.230. The court denies the request without prejudice to its renewal as a motion in limine to the trial court. The Mullenwegs seek monetary sanctions. The court orders Attayeb to pay $500 to the Mullenwegs within 15 days of entry of this order, finding her failure to comply with the discovery statutes noted above to lack substantial justification. The court denies the bulk of the Mullenwegs' request for sanctions for the reasons discussed in the court's order of May 6, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: LORRAINE DAVIS VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion And Motion For Protective Order By Cross-Defendant/Cross-Complainant, Fellow Industries, Inc., To Defendant/Cross-Complainant, City And County Of San Francisco'S Notice Of Site Inspection Of Property Located At 820 Valencia Street; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Request For Sanctions Of $2,450.00 Against Defendant/Cross-Complainant, City And County Of San Francisco'S And Its Counsel Of Record; Declaration Of Lisa Mersereau, Esq.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2025, line 6, CROSS COMPLAINANT FELLOW INDUSTRIES, INC.'S Motion And Motion For Protective Order By Cross-Defendant/Cross-Complainant, Fellow Industries, Inc., To Defendant/Cross-Complainant, City And County Of San Francisco'S Notice Of Site Inspection Of Property Located At 820 Valencia Street; Request For Sanctions Of $2,450.00 Against Defendant/Cross-Complainant, City And County Of San Francisco'S And Its Counsel Of Record Cross-defendant/cross-complainant Fellow Industries, Inc.'s (Fellow) motion for protective order is granted in part. Even though the proof of service for the amended notice of inspection was defective, the amended inspection demand was properly served. Fellow does not contend otherwise and admits to receiving it. (Johnston Decl., par. 2; Mersereau Decl., par. 4.) The amended inspection demand is proper under CCP 2031.010 and reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of relevant and admissible evidence (CCP 2017.010). The court does not see why the inspection needs to occur during work hours. The motion sufficiently makes clear the relief it seeks. Sanctions are denied as both sides acted with substantial justification. The inspection shall occur at 10:00 a.m. by May 27, 2025. Fellow to make sure that an employee arrives early for work to facilitate the inspection. Parties to meet and confer to coordinate precise date of inspection. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TERRY BROWN ET AL VS. WALGREENS BOOTS ALLIANCE, INC, A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For An In-Camera Hearing To Determine The Privileged San Francisco Police Department Investigative Records That Must Be Released To Plaintiffs Pursuant To The Courts Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2025, line 7, PLAINTIFF TERRY BROWN AS CO-SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO DECEDENT CHYNNA BROWN (A.K.A. BANKO BROWN), KEVINISHA HENDERSON'S Motion For An In-Camera Hearing To Determine The Privileged San Francisco Police Department Investigative Records That Must Be Released To Plaintiffs Pursuant To The Courts Protective Order (tentative ruling part 1 see part 2) Hearing required. Sgt. McWilliams's chronological report was not included in the USB drive lodged with the court. The court's tentative appears below. Plaintiffs Terry Brown et al. seek production of San Francisco Police Department records related to its investigation of the April 27, 2023 shooting that gives rise to this case. Plaintiffs and SFPD have stipulated that the court will review in camera those records to determine whether any should be released to Plaintiffs, applying the factors set out in Evidence Code section 1040 and applicable caselaw. Importantly, Plaintiffs have agreed to treat any disclosed information confidentially pursuant to a protective order entered in this court on December 9, 2024. (See Order of Dec. 9, 2024; Stipulation and Order, Apr. 22, 2025, p. 3:17-21.) Evidence Code section 1040(b)(2) requires the court to balance the need for confidentiality of official information against the public interest in disclosure. Here, the court orders disclosure only to Plaintiffs in confidence pursuant to the December 9 Order; any broader disclosure would require a further court order. The court finds that Plaintiffs' interest in the information is significant for the reasons stated in the parties' stipulation at 2:24-27. That significant need must be balanced against the official interests in secrecy. Here, that includes that the District Attorney could pursue charges related to the shooting in the future and that there is a general public interest in permitting investigating agencies to develop information in confidence. Those are important interests, but the court finds them somewhat attenuated for present purposes, because there is no indication that the investigating agency is actively involved in this case at present, and because the December 9 Order makes the disclosure here a limited one. Accordingly the court orders SFPD to disclose those items specified in the In Camera Review worksheets, a copy of which will be provided to SFPD and Plaintiffs' counsel at the May 13 hearing. The court appreciates the parties' cooperative efforts in narrowing the issues in dispute and presenting them cleanly to the court for resolution. (end of tentative ruling part 1, see part 2) = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: BONELLI ENTERPRISES, LLC, VS. TANTALUS BUILDERS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2025, line 16, CROSS DEFENDANT BONELLI ENTERPRISES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT Bonelli Enterprises, LLC's motion to strike Tantalus Builders, Inc.'s answer and cross-complaint in its entirety is granted. Tantalus has not obtained counsel and may not appear in the court through a non-attorney. "[T]he court retains authority to dismiss an action if an unrepresented corporation does not obtain counsel within reasonable time." (CLD Construction, Inc. v. City of San Ramon (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 1141, 1150.) Tantalus has been on notice of its need to obtain counsel since at least February 2025 and has not yet done so. rulings.Case Title: GABRIEL ESPINOZA VS. ILLINOIS TOOLS WORKS INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2025, line 10, PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. The court finds pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (f)(4), that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. Accordingly the motion is granted and the parties may submit the proposed judgment for signature. rulings.Case Title: CAMLO LOOPER VS. GARY M. WAITE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of The Court Record [Cal. R. Ct. 2.551(B)]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 13, 2025, line 11, PLAINTIFF CAMLO LOOPER'S Motion To Seal Portions Of The Court Record [Cal. R. Ct. 2.551(B)] The unopposed motion to seal portions of the court record in Waite v. Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Case No. CGC-24-613804, is granted. That case was filed but never served and was subsequently dismissed. Moving party Camlo Looper seeks an order that page 3, lines 6-8 and 18-24 ("Redacted Portions"), be redacted and removed from the public record. His motion has been served on the plaintiff in that case, Gary Waite. Pursuant to Rule of Court 2.550 and 2.551, the court grants the requested relief. The Redacted Portions contain salacious material that could have been stricken on motion and that, since the case was dismissed by the plaintiff before Looper or his employer could defend it on the merits, can no longer be addressed or rebutted through the judicial process. The court finds under the unusual circumstances here that there is exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the Redacted Portions, to wit Looper's interest in his good name and public presentation; that the overriding interest supports sealing the Redacted Portions; that a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; that the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and that no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. The clerk of this court is directed to place the complaint in Waite v. Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Case No. CGC-24-613804, under seal, and to substitute in its place the redacted version attached as Exhibit A to the Fenske Declaration. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
